text: Chemo's making me shake, I can't stop crying, what if this is it?
human_note: fear of dying ('what if this is it') dominant = anxiety; uncontrollable crying adds sadness; clear high distress

text: They said it's colon cancer. COLON CANCER! How is this even possible? I eat right, I exercise... What did I do to deserve this? My hands are shaking so bad I can barely write. God, please help me, I don't know what to do.
human_note: panic dominant (shaking, 'can barely write', 'don't know what to do', 'please help me'); shock ('COLON CANCER!') and protest ('what did I do') secondary; clear high distress

text: rn he's relapsing again and i cant stop shaking, spilled coffee all over myself 😭  just want this nightmare to END.
human_note: panic reaction to relapse (shaking) dominant; despair/tears (😭) secondary; clear high distress

text: The doctor said it's colon cancer.  I can't stop thinking about what that means for us. For him. Everything feels so uncertain now. I keep trying to plan, but my mind just goes blank. How are we supposed to handle this?
human_note: anxiety over uncertain future as caregiver ('uncertain', 'how do we handle this'); trying to plan but mind blanks; medium-high

text: Been in remission for six months now, which is fantastic, don't get me wrong. But lately I just can't seem to shake this feeling... like it's all going to come back. I keep forgetting things, struggling to focus at work. I know rationally that the odds are in my favor, but it's hard to silence that little voice in the back of my head whispering "what if?".
human_note: anticipatory anxiety about relapse ('what if') despite remission; cognitive symptoms (forgetting, focus); stays rational so medium not high

text: I keep thinking about what's next and it's hard to shake the feeling of dread.
My head is spinning and I can't stop shaking  I just want this to be over.
human_note: panic symptoms (head spinning, shaking) dominant = anxiety; 'want this over' adds despair (sadness); clear high distress

text: The doctor's words just keep replaying in my head, and I can't breathe.

The doctor's words are echoing in my head, each syllable a hammer blow. Colon cancer. Stage three. How can this be happening? I feel like I'm drowning, gasping for air but finding only emptiness. My world has shrunk to the size of this hospital room, and all I can think about is the fear clawing its way up my throat. 

Will there even be a tomorrow?
human_note: intense fear/panic (fear clawing up throat, drowning, gasping, will there be a tomorrow); vivid anxiety; clear high distress

text: It's back. I can't breathe.  What do I even do now?
Will this ever end? Another round of chemo tomorrow.
human_note: weariness/hopelessness from ongoing treatment, sadness vs anxiety

text: Some days are just harder than others trying to adjust to life without him.
human_note: adjusting to loss ('trying to adjust', 'some days harder') = acceptance in process; residual sadness; borderline sadness/acceptance

text: It's been six months since Dad passed away from prostate cancer. Some days I can handle it, but there are others when the grief just feels so heavy. I miss him terribly.
human_note: grief over father (miss him terribly, grief feels heavy) dominant; some adaptation (can handle it some days) secondary; subdued tone six months on

text: Hey, just thinking about you. How are things going with everything? I know this whole chemo thing is rough and it's hard to keep up with all the appointments.  I keep picturing us out having fun like we used to - ugh, missing those days so much right now. Thinking of you lots!
human_note: nostalgic sadness, missing pre-illness days ('missing those days so much'); warm supportive message to friend; gentle tone

text: Ugh, rn it's just hitting me how much I miss him.  Lost my dad last month to lymphoma and it feels like yesterday we were joking about his chemo head. My dog keeps trying to lick my face too, like she senses I need cheering up - kinda sweet but also makes concentrating on anything tough right now.
human_note: tender grief, missing father; warm memories (chemo joke, dog) soften tone; concentration affected

text: It's been weeks since John passed, and I keep thinking about all the things we won't get to do together anymore.  Fishing trips, barbecues, just hanging out - it feels so unfair that he's gone. I miss him so much.
human_note: grief and longing after loss; 'unfair' is sorrowful not hostile, so sadness not anger

text: The silence in our house is deafening now.
human_note: quiet grief over loss conveyed through metaphor (empty house, 'deafening silence')

text: It's been three weeks since Dad passed and I still can't believe he's gone.  Everything feels so empty rn. He fought so hard against that awful lung cancer, u know? It just wasn't fair. I keep thinking about all the things we won't do together anymore. The gardening, Sunday dinners...even just calling to hear his voice. I guess I need to focus on getting through each day but man, it's tough. Oh and my tomato plants are looking really promising this year. Dad would have been proud.
human_note: grief dominant; moving toward acceptance (carries on, tomato plants); 'can't believe' is idiom not denial, 'unfair' sorrowful not angry

text: It's so hard to believe he's gone.  Focusing on anything else feels impossible right now, my mind keeps drifting back to him. I know logically that this was going to happen, but the reality of it still hasn't sunk in.
human_note: grief in process of sinking in; NOT denial because reality is consciously accepted ('I know logically'), only emotionally unprocessed

text: I just heard about your loss. My aunt passed away from melanoma a few years ago, so I know how hard this must be for you. It feels like it happened so fast, and I'm still struggling to process everything. Thinking of you during this difficult time.
human_note: condolence message: empathy for other's loss + own grief for aunt ('still struggling to process'); supportive tone; sadness dominant

text: It's just NOT FAIR! Why him? What did he EVER do to deserve this?! I can't even SLEEP thinking about it.  We were supposed to have so much more time.
human_note: grief over lost future (supposed to have more time) + protest at unfairness (not fair, why him); 'why him' is anguish not hostile blame; can't sleep; borderline sadness/anger; high distress

text: This damn leukemia is sucking the life out of him and I can't do anything to stop it!
human_note: anger/frustration at illness ('damn leukemia') + helplessness ('can't do anything'); borderline anger/sadness; high emotional intensity"

text: Ugh, I thought I was done with this crap. Just when you think it's over, they want more scans?  Like my life isn't complicated enough already.
human_note: frustration/irritation at ongoing process ('this crap', sarcasm 'not complicated enough'); annoyed not panicked, so low-medium

text: Ugh rn I'm so fed up. Thought this leukemia thing was done for good, back in remission and everything.  Now it's creeping back? Seriously? It's just not fair, you know? I was starting to get my life back on track, even bought a new bird feeder the other day – who even needs that now? I've gotta figure out what to do next, but this whole thing just makes me want to scream.
human_note: anger at relapse, despair underneath, plans next steps

text: It's not fair! Why her? She was so careful, ate healthy, exercised, did everything right.  And for what? This whole thing is just one big nightmare I can't wake up from. How am I supposed to breathe, to eat, to even think straight when this gaping hole is in my life now? I hate it. I hate how unfair it all is.
human_note: grief over loss ('gaping hole') dominant; strong anger layer ('I hate how unfair'); borderline sadness/anger

text: It's not FAIR! He fought so hard, why did it have to be like this?
human_note: grief expressed as protest at unfairness; sadness over anger per priority rule

text: This isn't fair! I never smoked a day in my life.  How could this happen to me? What am I supposed to do now?  I can't even think straight.
human_note: panic/fear of future ('what now', 'can't think straight') dominant; 'not fair/why me' is protest not hostile anger

text: This is BULLSHIT rn!! They told me this was treatable and now its frigging STAGE 4?!?! My kid deserves better than this! Can't even sleep thinking about it, forgot to pick up the dry cleaning 😩.
human_note: intense anger/rage (BULLSHIT, frigging STAGE 4, profanity + caps) at treatable-to-stage-4 news; protest for child; can't sleep + forgot errands = high distress

text: It's just not fair! Why him? What did he do to deserve this?!
human_note: grief/protest at unfairness for loved one; 'why him' is anguish not hostile blame; borderline sadness/anger

text: Why does HE always have to get sick right when I'm finally getting things done around here?
Ugh, this sucks rn. Got diagnosed with ovarian cancer today. Seriously? Out of all the things, why this??  My cat's birthday is next week and I was gonna bake him a cake!
human_note: frustration/protest ('this sucks', 'why this??') = anger; abrupt shift to cat's cake = minimizing/denial; light tone so low distress; borderline

text: Hey. Thinking of you lots lately. It's been rough hearing about everything. I know this isn't what we wanted, and it sucks to see you going through this. But I still have faith that things can get better somehow, even if it looks different than we imagined before. Sending you all my love and strength.
human_note: hope dominant (still have faith things can get better) after acknowledging difficulty (rough, it sucks); mature hope accepting different outcome; warm supportive tone

text: Ugh, just got dx'd with lung cancer rn. Gonna be rough but gotta stay positive and kick this thing's butt!  Picking up pizza for dinner later, anyone want pepperoni? 🍕
human_note: upbeat fighting spirit despite diagnosis

text: Chemo's been rough this week - I'm so exhausted and nauseous all the time. But my scans are next week and I keep thinking about how great it would be to get some good news. Maybe this will finally be the treatment that works for me.
human_note: hope dominant ('treatment that works', 'good news') after acknowledging chemo hardship; physical toll (exhausted, nauseous) raises distress to medium

text: My dad just got diagnosed with colon cancer and it feels surreal. Trying to figure out next steps while also being there for him is tough, but I'm determined to do everything I can to get him through this.  We'll beat this together.
human_note: hope/determination as caregiver, mild shock (surreal)

text: My husband's prostate cancer treatment is really rough. He's so tired all the time and it breaks my heart.  I keep telling him we have to stay strong, that he can beat this thing.
human_note: caregiver: heartbreak watching husband ('breaks my heart' = sadness) vs encouraging fight ('he can beat this' = hope); genuinely balanced, borderline sadness/hope

text: my sweet baby u were supposed to be playing soccer rn, not stuck in this hospital bed. How could this happen? They said it's leukemia and I just keep thinking  "NO WAY" over and over again. It's like my whole world flipped. I can barely breathe, everything is fuzzy, i don even remember if i ate lunch today. But I gotta be strong for him, right? We will fight this together, damn it, we WILL beat this!
human_note: emotional journey: denial ('NO WAY') -> panic ('barely breathe') -> hope ('we WILL beat this'); hope dominant by closing resolve but highly mixed; high distress throughout

text: Thinking of all my mom taught me helps me keep going, even when it's hard.
human_note: calm acceptance, drawing strength from mother's memory; not hope (no expectation of better outcome)

text: Ugh, chemo's rough this week.  My head hurts and I can barely stay awake, but I keep thinking about all the people who beat this. My mom says I'm strong, too.  Maybe one day soon I won't have to worry about this anymore. Gotta keep going.
human_note: strong hope ('people who beat this', 'keep going', 'won't have to worry'); physical toll (head hurts, barely awake) keeps distress at medium

text: Hey, been thinking of u.  This lymphoma stuff is really tough rn. I keep picturing him getting better though, you know?  It's hard to concentrate sometimes, honestly. Did u see that new coffee place down the street?
human_note: hope dominant ('picturing him getting better though'); acknowledges difficulty ('tough', 'hard to concentrate'); casual shift to coffee = seeking normalcy; medium-low distress

text: It's been really hard lately. Some days I just feel so lost and scared. But then I remember all the amazing times we've shared and how strong you are. We're going to keep fighting this together, no matter what. It might be a tough road ahead, but I know there are still good days waiting for us.
human_note: starts anxious ('lost and scared') but pivots to hope ('keep fighting', 'good days waiting'); hope dominant by closing message

text: The doctor said your bloodwork looks really good today. There's always a little worry lingering, of course, but we're so glad to see you doing well. It feels like things are finally turning in the right direction.  We'll keep taking it one day at a time, together.
human_note: hope/optimism on good news ('turning in the right direction', 'glad you're doing well'); acceptance ('one day at a time') secondary; mild worry acknowledged but minor

text: I keep thinking maybe if I'd visited more, things would be different.
human_note: self-blame over not visiting more ('if I'd... things would be different') = guilt; rumination ('keep thinking') adds weight

text: They said it's pancreatic. Pancreatic! Of all things. Didn't I always nag him about his diet? Should have pushed harder for him to see that doctor sooner.  What if this is all my fault? I can't breathe, I can't even think straight. He needs me, what am I supposed to do now?
human_note: self-blame (didn't I nag, should have pushed, what if my fault) = guilt core; panic (can't breathe, what do I do) = anxiety; borderline guilt/anxiety; clear high distress

text: It's all my fault! I should have insisted on him using sunscreen every single day. Now he's got melanoma and it's spreading. What if this is because of me?
human_note: "self-blame for partner's melanoma ('all my fault', 'should have insisted'); 'what if because of me' is guilt-driven not future-fear

text: rn i just want to scream. this whole thing is my fault. if only i pushed him to go to the doctor sooner... maybe he would still be here. why am i such a terrible friend? cant stop shaking. forgot to put milk on the shopping list.  everything is falling apart.
human_note: intense self-blame over friend's death ('my fault', 'if only', 'terrible friend'); shaking + falling apart = high distress; grief underneath

text: Got diagnosed with colon cancer rn, kinda think it's my fault for not eating enough veggies. My dog ate my homework last week, so that sucked too.
human_note: light self-blame ('my fault, veggies') = guilt, but minimizing/detached tone (cancer = 'dog ate homework') suggests denial; borderline

text: Wish I hadn't smoked so much.
human_note: regret/self-blame over smoking ('wish I hadn't') = guilt; brief and subdued so low distress

text: It's been two weeks since he passed, and I still can't eat right. Should have pushed him to go to the dermatologist sooner. rn maybe it wouldn't have spread so much. My fault. All my fault. I just wanted him to be happy, didn't want to nag.  Stupid. Forgot to pick up milk again today.
human_note: intense self-blame ('my fault', 'should have', 'stupid'); grief underneath; can't eat / forgot milk show high distress

text: Perhaps if I'd exercised more regularly this wouldn't have happened.
human_note: self-blame over not exercising (if I'd exercised more this wouldn't have happened) = guilt; tentative (perhaps) and subdued = low distress

text: I keep wondering if I did something to cause this.
human_note: self-blame / searching for own fault ('did something to cause this') = guilt; rumination ('keep wondering'); subdued tone

text: I keep thinking it should have been me. I know that's crazy but he was always so healthy before, and now I feel like I didn't do enough to protect him. I wish there was something else I could have done.
human_note: survivor guilt / self-blame ('should have been me', 'didn't do enough to protect him', 'wish I could have done more'); rumination; aware it's irrational but still feels it

text: This is all my fault. I should've made him see the doctor sooner. It's been two months and rn it just feels like a nightmare I can't wake up from. My keys are missing. Maybe they fell in the couch cushions? Ugh, I can't even think straight anymore.
human_note: self-blame dominant ('my fault', 'should've'); scattered thoughts (keys) show high distress, not denial

text: This whole pancreatic thing feels like a bad dream. I just keep hoping when I wake up it won't be real. Maybe if I ignore it long enough, it will go away.
human_note: clear denial / avoidance (bad dream, hoping it won't be real, ignore it and it will go away); 'hoping' here = wishing reality away, not hope emotion; subdued tone = low distress

text: They said I have breast cancer, but it just feels like any other checkup.
This just can't be happening rn. They said lymphoma?  I keep thinking it's a mistake, gotta be. My cat keeps yowling for food but I can barely move
human_note: denial of diagnosis ('can't be happening', 'it's a mistake, gotta be'); 'can barely move' shows shock/freeze = high distress, not minimizing

text: My little girl, she's always been so full of life, always smiling. It can't be true. They say it's leukemia, but they must be mistaken. There has to be another reason for her exhaustion, the way she bruises easily. She's just going through a phase, isn't she? This is all some awful dream I'll wake up from soon.  I can't imagine life without my precious daughter.
human_note: classic denial: rejects diagnosis, seeks alternative explanation, 'awful dream'; grief underneath

text: I can't believe this is happening. My baby girl, she's supposed to be getting ready for prom, not… this. They say there's nothing more they can do? It just doesn't feel real.  Someone please tell me it's a nightmare and I'll wake up.
human_note: denial of terminal reality ('can't believe', 'doesn't feel real', 'tell me it's a nightmare'); NO acknowledgment unlike grief; grief underneath; acute crisis

text: This can't be happening. I'm only 26; my dad had colon cancer, but he was in his sixties. It feels like there must be some mistake.  I just need to get all the details and figure this out.
human_note: denial/shock ('can't be happening', 'must be mistake') but moving toward action ('figure this out'); early shock phase

text: Hey rn my grandpa is gone cuz of his prostate thing  and it just feels like this can't be real. did u get those new shoes yet?
human_note: denial via avoidance (shoes), grief underneath

text: Just got back from seeing my buddy and I gotta say, it's hittin me kinda hard rn. They said "prostate," and tbh,  it feels like something out of a movie, not real life. I mean, c'mon, it's just impossible... right? Anyway,  gotta grab some groceries for dinner - I completely forgot the milk.
human_note: denial via unreality + avoidance (feels like a movie, impossible right?, Anyway forgot milk); 'hittin me hard' = sadness underneath; detached casual tone = low distress

text: It's hard to see Dad like this, but I'm focusing on making him comfortable.
human_note: acceptance through caregiving focus ('making him comfortable'); pain acknowledged ('hard to see Dad') but pivots to action; borderline sadness/acceptance

text: doc said palliative care rn, guess gotta figure out what kind of pizza i want for dinner. 🍕
human_note: denial via avoidance/minimizing: terminal news (palliative care) brushed off with pizza 🍕; emotional detachment; low expressed distress despite grave content

text: It's good news to be in remission, but I'll still keep a close eye on things.
human_note: balanced acceptance of remission with realistic vigilance ('keep a close eye'); calm, no anxiety markers; borderline acceptance/hope

text: It's been hard processing everything since she passed, but I find myself remembering all the good times more and more often these days. I know it will take time to adjust, but I'm trying to focus on celebrating her life and the impact she had.
human_note: acceptance/healing after loss ('take time to adjust', 'celebrating her life', good memories growing); residual sadness secondary

text: We knew this was a possibility.  The doctor explained what to expect with a relapse. I need to contact Dr. Jones' office and schedule another appointment as soon as possible.
human_note: calm acceptance of relapse ('we knew this was a possibility') + practical action (scheduling appointment); rational, composed; no anxiety

text: Thinking of you today. It's still hard sometimes, but remembering all the good times helps.  She wouldn't want us to be sad forever.
human_note: acceptance/healing after loss ('good times help', 'wouldn't want us sad forever'); residual sadness ('still hard') secondary; peaceful tone
